mxfile.agent: Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/145.0.0.0 Safari/537.36 Edg/145.0.0.0

mxCell.value:
VWAN Hub with Palo
Germany West Central
FKB–SCP-App1-Prod

Config:
Internet Route for Application Gateway
WAF enabled

Web Application Traffic

Public Application Gateway

The default traffic for the public application gateway subnets will be routed to the Azure default gateway. Futhermore, the application gateway is assigned a public IP address as a frontend. In the backend, the application gateway points directly to the backend systems. The traffic is not routed through the Palo Alto firewalls because the corresponding security measures are taken over by the application gateway. An enforcement of prevention mode is a must. Exceptions for not using prevention mode can only be approved by the Security Team. The application subnets still have the normal routes propagated by Virtual WAN hubs. Application gateways control only traffic that uses the HTTP/HTTPS protocol.

The following diagram illustrates the communication between application gateway, central firewalls and the actual workload:

VWAN Hub with Palo
Germany West Central
FKB–SCP-App1-Prod

Internal Application Gateway

Internal-facing only web applications should make use of a web application gateway, which should remain inaccessible to the public. The default route is propagated by the Virtual WAN Hub:

VWAN Hub with Palo
Germany West Central
FKB–SCP-App1-Prod

Non Web Application Traffic

Routing via the Palo Alto firewalls, therefore via the central network infrastructure, must always be ensured for non-web application traffic. This applies both to traffic from the internal network and to traffic from the Internet. An example for both scenarios looks like this:

VWAN Hub with Palo
Germany West Central
FKB–SCP-App1-Prod

Intune 
MDM mgd
Mobile device
MS Defender client VPN

Windows 
Client
PA GlobalProtect VPN client

VWAN Hub with Palo
Germany West Central
FKB–SCP-App1-Prod

Non Web Application Traffic

Routing via the Palo Alto firewalls, therefore via the central network infrastructure, must always be ensured for non-web application traffic. This applies both to traffic from the internal network and to traffic from the Internet. An example for both scenarios looks like this:
